seoTitle: How to Get the Outlook QR Code for Mobile Sign-In
metaDescription: Find the login QR code in Outlook desktop or web to instantly pair your phone's Outlook Mobile app without typing your password on a small screen.
intro: Typing a long corporate password on a phone keyboard is tedious, and Outlook offers a faster way to link your mobile app. This guide covers finding the QR pairing option from the Help menu, scanning it with your phone's camera, and completing any required verification step.

steps.heading: Open Outlook desktop or web
steps.body: Launch either your Outlook desktop app or your webmail workspace interface.

steps.heading: Go to the Help tab
steps.body: Move to the top ribbon menu and click on the Help tab.

steps.heading: Select Outlook Mobile
steps.body: Choose the Outlook Mobile option to launch the identity connection panel.

steps.heading: Wait for the QR code
steps.body: Let the cloud sync window generate a unique temporary QR code.

steps.heading: Scan with your phone
steps.body: Open Outlook on your Android or iPhone, tap the QR icon to wake the camera, and scan the code on your screen.

steps.heading: Complete verification
steps.body: Accept the device pairing prompt and enter a multi-factor code if your organization requires it.

faq.question: Does the QR code expire?
faq.answer: Yes, the temporary QR code is time-limited, so if the pairing sequence times out, you'll need to refresh it and scan again.

faq.question: Is scanning the QR code more secure than typing a password?
faq.answer: It's designed as a convenient passwordless pairing method, though your organization may still require multi-factor verification for added security.

faq.question: What if my phone's camera won't scan the code?
faq.answer: Make sure the Outlook mobile app has camera permissions enabled and that your screen brightness is high enough for a clean scan.

faq.question: Will my folders and tags sync after pairing?
faq.answer: Yes, after a successful pairing you should check your phone's inbox to confirm your custom tags and folders render correctly.
